food_subgroup: Grain-based bowl meal with spiced meat and baked dessert component

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite commercial meal combining a grain bowl, spicy beef with taco seasoning, and a cookie. FOODON_00002473 (beef-based dish) used as closest parent for the protein component. The full composite meal has no exact FoodOn representation.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Beef meat dish) used as closest parent. The composite meal includes grain bowl base, spicy beef with taco seasoning, and a cookie; no single FoodEx2 code covers this combination. Cookie component would separately map to confectionery category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Beef dish) used as closest parent for the dominant protein component. The composite meal structure is not represented in SNOMED CT.

main_ingredients: taco seasoning blend (chilli, cumin, paprika, garlic)
allergens: gluten (wheat in cookie and possible grain base)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a grain-based energy bowl with spicy beef and taco seasoning plus one cookie, as served in a Singapore field survey meal set.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Composite estimation from beef taco bowl components and sugar cookie
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for this commercial composite meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using USDA FoodData Central entries: (1) Beef, ground, 85% lean, cooked, pan-browned (FDC ID 748967) for the spicy beef component; (2) Rice, white, cooked (FDC ID 169756) for the grain bowl base; (3) Mixed salad greens, raw (FDC ID 169248) for the vegetable component; (4) Cookies, sugar, commercially prepared (FDC ID 174928) for the cookie component. Approximate component weight split: beef 35%, rice base 30%, vegetables 15%, cookie 20%. Values represent a blended per-100g estimate for the full meal set at approximately 450g total serving weight. Taco seasoning sodium contribution estimated from USDA seasoning data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal); University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite commercial meal. GI estimated as a weighted average of components: white rice GI ~72 (Atkinson et al. 2021, University of Sydney GI Database), ground beef GI ~0 (protein/fat food, no glycemic response), sugar cookie GI ~55 (Atkinson et al. 2021), mixed vegetables GI ~15. Weighted by carbohydrate contribution across components. Blended meal GI estimated at approximately 56 (medium category). Glycemic load per 450g serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 100.8) / 100 ≈ 56.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from taco seasoning blend and processed components
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to an estimated sodium content of approximately 310 mg per 100g (approximately 1,395 mg per full 450g serving), which represents over 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg for hypertensive patients; request reduced seasoning or substitute with a lower-sodium protein option.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index driven by white rice base and cookie component, with moderate total carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this meal due to its medium GI of approximately 56 and a high glycemic load of approximately 56.4 per serving; substituting the white rice base with a lower-GI grain such as brown rice or quinoa and omitting the cookie would substantially reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef and cookie butter content, combined with elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption of this meal due to its estimated saturated fat content of approximately 2.9 g per 100g (approximately 13 g per serving) and high sodium load; choosing leaner protein alternatives and omitting the cookie would improve the cardiovascular risk profile of this meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Energy Bowl with Spicy Beef, Taco Seasoning, and Cookie is a composite Western-style commercial meal available on field survey data, served by restaurants including Subway Funan and Funan Mix & Match outlets. The meal consists of a grain-based energy bowl (typically white rice or a mixed grain base) topped with seasoned ground beef prepared with a taco spice blend containing chilli, cumin, paprika, and garlic, accompanied by fresh mixed salad vegetables, and served with a sweet baked cookie as a dessert component. The beef is typically grilled or pan-sautéed and carries a moderately spicy, smoky flavour profile with savoury umami notes from the taco seasoning. The cookie adds a sweet, buttery contrast to the savoury bowl. Nutritionally, the meal provides a moderate energy density of approximately 198 kcal per 100g, with meaningful protein from the beef component (approximately 11.2 g per 100g), moderate carbohydrate from the grain base and cookie (approximately 22.4 g per 100g), and moderate fat (approximately 7.8 g per 100g). The sodium content is elevated due to the taco seasoning blend, making this meal a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 56 (medium category), driven primarily by the white rice base and the cookie. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and consider lower-GI grain substitutions. The meal is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy allergies without modification. It is a popular fast-casual meal format in Singapore's food delivery ecosystem, reflecting the fusion of Western Tex-Mex flavours with the local bowl-meal dining culture.

confidence_reason: This is a commercial composite meal sold on field survey data with no direct entry in any approved primary nutritional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using USDA FoodData Central reference entries for individual ingredients. The exact recipe, portion weights, seasoning quantities, and cookie specifications vary by restaurant and are not publicly disclosed. Confidence is therefore low. GI is an analogous estimate based on component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021.
notes: 1. This record represents a composite commercial meal (GD000387) sold on field survey data by Subway Funan and Funan Mix & Match. The food name in the input ('Energy Bowl Spicy Beef Taco Cookie Meal') has been normalised to 'Energy Bowl with Spicy Beef, Taco Seasoning, and Cookie' for canonical clarity. 2. The input cuisine tag 'Sandwich' and food_group 'Desserts' from the seed record are incorrect for this composite meal; cuisine has been corrected to 'Western' and food_group to 'Mixed'. 3. All nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data; no exact match exists in any approved primary source. 4. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for estimation and is not reliably present in the meal components at measurable levels. 5. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and carry low confidence. 6. Halal status is marked false as beef sourcing and preparation halal certification cannot be confirmed from available data. 7. The price of SGD 15.20 is preserved from the input source (field survey data). 8. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the meal is served in Singapore. 9. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this meal does not exist in any approved source.

Iteration 1: Constructed component-weighted nutrient estimates from USDA FoodData Central with documented FDC IDs and weight splits.
Iteration 1: Added all five mandatory Singaporean multilingual names (Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Iteration 1: Documented vitamin_d_mcg as -1 with explicit note explaining it is not reported in source data.
Iteration 1: Set glycemic_index to 56 (numeric >0) with analogous estimate documentation from Atkinson et al. 2021 and University of Sydney GI Database.
Iteration 1: Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency — all multilingual alias strings confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases array.
Iteration 1: Used only allowed ontology match_type values (closest_parent) with gap notes for all three ontology systems.
